answer:
~$0 federal tax. Shares acquired 2016 (post-9/27/2010) qualify for 100% Section 1202 exclusion; 5-year holding met. $8M gain is under your $10M-or-10x-basis cap, so fully excluded — no capital gains tax, no AMT preference, no 3.8% NIIT on excluded gain.

Caveats that could change this:
- Exclusion assumes all QSBS requirements actually hold: original issuance from the C-corp (not bought from another shareholder), corp had ≤$50M gross assets at issuance, active qualified trade/business, no disqualifying redemptions. Get the company's QSBS attestation and have a tax pro confirm.
- State tax separate — CA, PA, NJ, etc. don't conform; state liability could be significant depending on residence.
- Any gain above the cap (not your case) would be taxed at 23.8–31.8%.
